lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: How are you getting on, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I be looking for again?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: You should look anywhere Glough may have left behind clues of his whereabouts. I've already had my men search his home, but perhaps you can find something they missed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there, King Narnode.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Good of you to stop by, human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How fares the kingdom today?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Everything seems fine, as far as I can tell... I will let you know if there is any trouble.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: All is not well, [player name]. Despite having had Glough under close surveillance since the events on Ape Atoll, he has managed to escape the Stronghold.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I know you have already done so much for our kingdom, but could you look into Glough's disappearance?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll help you look for Glough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea on where to start?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: You should look anywhere Glough may have left behind clues of his whereabouts. I've already had my men search his home, but perhaps you can find something they missed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right to it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm busy at the moment.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Perhaps some other time, then.

sections.title: Investigating the tree in Glough's home

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder who could have left this in Glough's home? The initials A.A. are embroidered into it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is this your handkerchief?

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: Why, yes, it is. I must have left it behind somewhere...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Anita.

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: *sob* W-what do you want? *sob*

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: *sob* It's Glough... I fear for the worst!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: I think he might be seeing someone else! *sob* I hadn't heard from him in a while, and so decided to give him a visit.

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: At first, I thought there was nobody home... *sob* But then I heard... *sob*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Heard what?

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: I've already said too much. *sob* The last time I helped you, Glough lost his job!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you care? He's seeing someone else!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He might be in trouble, I could help.

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: I heard whispering coming from higher up in his tree! I managed to climb the first branch... *sob* But I'm not agile enough to get up to the highest level...

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: Do you really think he might have gotten into trouble? *sob*

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's another level to his home?

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: *sob* There's nowhere else the voices could have been coming from... *sob* Please, climb up there and let me know what you find!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea where Glough is?

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: The last time I helped you... *sob* Glough lost his job!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing, goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The note is blank... I'm not sure what I'm meant to be reading right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What could the note mean? I should show it to the King, he might be able to make some sense of it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to translate this before it's going to be of any use... Maybe the King can help.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: How are you getting on, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found a note in Glough's home which might be of use.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Excellent, do you have it with you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have it right here.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Ah, I see that it is written in the ancient gnome language... Perhaps you could translate it with that translation book you've used before?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have a copy of the translation book any more.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Here's a spare copy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll give it a go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll give it a go.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: How are you getting on, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's too much missing from this translation book to make much sense of the note.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: It is true that the translation guide is incomplete... Perhaps it would be of more use to go to the author directly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see if the author can help translate the note for me, then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Anita.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you translate these notes? I found them in Glough's home, half of it is missing.

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: Hmm... This certainly isn't Glough's handwriting...

lines.speaker: Anita
lines.text: Here you go, hopefully it helps you find Glough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What could the note mean? I should show it to the King, he might be able to make some sense of it.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Have you been able to find out where Glough has gone, [player name]

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, King Narnode.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Please hurry, [player name]. Glough is very dangerous and must be stopped!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Narnode! With Anita's help I was able to translate the note... It appears Glough had help leaving the stronghold!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: What!? Who could have committed such a crime? Show me the note.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: The note refers to a replacement pilot, we've only had one pilot reassigned recently and that's Lori taking over Le Smith's post as a balloon pilot.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: [player name], you must find out where Glough might have travelled to. Assistant Lori may be able to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right to it.

lines.speaker: Assistant Lori
lines.text: Do you want to use the balloon? Just so you know, some locations require special logs and high Firemaking skills.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Assistant Lori
lines.text: I am Assistant Lori. I've been assigned to the stronghold by Auguste.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to Assistant Le Smith?

lines.speaker: Assistant Lori
lines.text: I'm not sure, you would have to speak to Auguste about that.

lines.speaker: Assistant Lori
lines.text: Do you want to use the balloon?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Hello, [player name]. It is good to see you again. My enterprise has grown since we last met. I've expanded all over Gielinor!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: If you'd like to go to a new location, you will have to help fly the balloon again. Some of the routes are very dangerous!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: But each route you complete will earn you Firemaking experience.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Once you've opened a route, it only costs 1 log of the corresponding type to travel there again, from any location you've already opened!

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: I have also hired assistants to watch over the balloons at each new location we go to. They will help you get where you want to go.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Do you want to use the balloon? Just so you know, some locations require special logs and high Firemaking skills.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd like to talk about Assistant Le Smith.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: What a coincidence, he just so happened to visit recently. What's this about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to know about the route you taught him.

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Ah... I insisted it was far too dangerous, nobody has attempted to make that journey before!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Ape Atoll...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you teach me the route?

lines.speaker: Auguste
lines.text: Most certainly not! For all I know, I've already sent one of my finest pilots to his peril!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I have given you orders, [player name]. Report to Sergeant Garkor on Ape Atoll for further instruction.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: How are you getting on, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've found evidence that Glough may have headed to Ape Atoll.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: How could this be?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Assistant Le Smith is involved, it appears he has flown them there.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Agh! We were foolish to allow him to stay here after dismissing him from the glider regiment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what should we do now?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Glough must be working with the monkeys once again. Fortunately, the rest of the 10th squad remained on Ape Atoll after your previous mission, they have been keeping a close eye on Awowogei.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Perhaps they will have spotted something out of the ordinary that could help indicate what Glough is doing.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Glough is a threat to us all... I must hereby re-assign you to Ape Atoll, [player name]. Report to Sergeant Garkor on Ape Atoll for further orders.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Have you learned anything from Awowogei yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet...

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: We can't give up, [player name], come back when you've got more information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get to it right away.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Welcome back, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Narnode has sent me. I'm investigating the disappearance of Glough.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Glough... really? We've not had any indication that he has returned to Ape Atoll.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: But we have been seeing a lot of activity from Awowogei and his commanders - I assumed the king would send someone soon enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea what they're up to?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: We're not entirely sure, he has been keeping a tight lid on his plans. I guess it could be tied to Glough's disappearance? Perhaps you could get more information from Awowogei directly...

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: He may be willing to share his plans with you, assuming you are disguised with a greegree, that is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds far too dangerous.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: A member of the 10th squad should never back down in the face of adversity, come back when you're feeling up to the task.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's worth a shot.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Good luck, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you still doing here?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: My squad and I are still carrying out reconnaissance. Awowogei has been very occupied as of lately, could this have something to do with why you're here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm only passing through.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm only passing through.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: What do you want? I'm very busy right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are things going, Awowogei?

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: I don't have time for idle chat! What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm here to discuss military strategies.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: I only discuss such matters with my highest ranking military officials. In fact, I am supposed to be in a meeting with my commander right now. Kruk has been consistently late to our meetings recently.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Uwogo, have a messenger fetch him from his post.

lines.speaker: Uwogo
lines.text: I'm afraid all of our messengers are busy.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Hmmm, perhaps you could make yourself useful after all, visitor. Why don't you run along to the gate and remind Kruk of our meeting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, my king.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Garkor isn't going to be happy when I tell him about this...

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: What was that? Is there a problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: None at all.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Excellent. I must prepare for my meeting, Uwogo.

lines.speaker: Uwogo
lines.text: I'm afraid your time with the king is up, you should fetch Kruk from his post on the gate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't take orders from you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea where I can find Glough?

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: I don't know what you're talking about. We haven't been involved with Glough since he failed us.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Have you learned anything from Awowogei yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on getting that greegree.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Please hurry [player name], we need this information from Awowogei before it's too late.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get to it right away.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Have you learned anything from Awowogei yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He won't discuss his plans with anyone but his military commanders.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Ah, the monkey has more sense than we give him credit for... Perhaps you could go back in there as one of his commanders?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Zooknock could get you disguised as one of Awowogei's commanders if you retrieved the necessary items for him to make a greegree. We just need to choose which commander to go after...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well Awowogei mentioned something about a meeting with Kruk. He wanted me to go to find him.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Kruk eh... Captain of the monkey guard would certainly be a useful disguise to have at our disposal. Hopefully you can find him somewhere isolated from the rest of the guards... Once you have defeated him, take his

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: corpse to Zooknock along with an uncharged monkey talisman, well, you know the routine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get to it right away.

sections.title: Talking to the Monkey Archer again

lines.speaker: Monkey Archer
lines.text: Any luck finding Kruk?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Monkey Archer
lines.text: You'd best not keep the king waiting, try tracking down Kruk's movements.

lines.speaker: Monkey Archer
lines.text: There's only one way off the wall, and that's down the Western ramp to the shore. I know that's where I'd start looking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Monkey Archer
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The king has sent me with a message for Kruk, any idea where I can find him?

lines.speaker: Monkey Archer
lines.text: Your guess is as good as mine, you didn't hear it from me, but he's been disappearing from his post a lot lately...

lines.speaker: Monkey Archer
lines.text: You might be able to track him down if you've got what it takes, although I wouldn't want to be the one caught sneaking up on Kruk!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea where to start looking?

lines.speaker: Monkey Archer
lines.text: There's only one way off the wall, and that's down the Western ramp to the shore. I know that's where I'd start looking.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Stop right there, Kruk!

lines.speaker: Kruk
lines.text: I'm impressed you made it so far, human.

lines.speaker: Kruk
lines.text: However, I'm afraid that this is the end of the line for you.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Ah finally, Kruk! I was beginning to think you had run into trouble...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... No, everything is fine.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Excellent. Have you had any further considerations for our plans?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And which plans would we be talking about here?

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Are you sure everything is okay Kruk? You seem very distracted lately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm fine, just been very busy thinking over those plans. Could you remind me where we left off last time?

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: We decided to move forward with our three-way assault.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: You were negotiating an alliance with the ogres and trolls, working on a new fleet of battleships and then developing that secret weapon of yours.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: So, how is progress of our plans coming along?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Don't let me down Kruk, we all know what is at stake here.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: You must convince the trolls and ogres not to ally with the monkeys.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas on how?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: You should pay a visit to the military officials of each race. I believe a troll general by the name of Kob is still filling that role in the Troll Stronghold. As for the ogres, I would recommend finding the head of the city

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: guard in Gu'Tanoth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right to it.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Have you learned anything from Awowogei yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The monkeys are planning another attack!

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Ah, this comes as little surprise. Did you uncover any details of the plans?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The assault is coming in three stages...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: First, the monkeys wish to join into an alliance with the trolls and ogres...

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: That makes sense, it's no secret that those races are no fans of humans, other than perhaps as ingredients in a soup... What else did you learn?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The monkeys have been building a fleet of battleships.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: That is odd, we have spies posted at the shipyard who haven't reported any news of this to us. I'll get a few scouts to look into it further. What's the final element to this attack?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Apparently Kruk has been working on a secret weapon, I've got no idea what it is.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Well then, that sounds ominous. I'll have some men look into it. For now, let's deal with the problems we can solve.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: You must convince the trolls and ogres not to ally with the monkeys.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas on how?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: You should pay a visit to the military officials of each race. I believe a troll general by the name of Kob is still filling that role in the Troll Stronghold. As for the ogres, I would recommend finding the head of the city

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: guard in Gu'Tanoth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right to it.

lines.speaker: Kob
lines.text: What you do here, human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know about your deal with the monkeys. I'm here to persuade you not to join their alliance.

lines.speaker: Kob
lines.text: Alliance? We plan to crush the hairy little men as soon as the humans are gone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You won't be around to crush anyone when I'm done with you.

lines.speaker: Kob
lines.text: Ah, this food thinks it's a brave one, this will be fun!

lines.speaker: Kob
lines.text: Here's the game, I kill you, we help little hairy men kill all the humans. You kill me, we call off the attack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I accept your challenge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Kob
lines.text: Maybe human not as brave as Kob thinks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would it take to persuade you not to attack the humans?

lines.speaker: Kob
lines.text: Humans are all so weak! There is nothing you can offer us to stop the attack!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Kob
lines.text: I am Kob, mightiest of the troll generals!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll just be on my way.

lines.speaker: Kob
lines.text: Hurry before I get hungry!

lines.speaker: Kob
lines.text: Wait! Maybe battle to death is too much...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about our deal?

lines.speaker: Kob
lines.text: You win, I will call off the attack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for the head of the city guard...

lines.speaker: Keef
lines.text: You found him! What do you want, little human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know about your deal with the monkeys. I'm here to persuade you not to join their alliance.

lines.speaker: Keef
lines.text: Hahaha! Monkeys offer us treasure, land and all the humans we can eat! What do you offer us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I could offer you more?

lines.speaker: Keef
lines.text: I don't think human can offer me cities like the monkey king can!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... that's not going to work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I offer to spare your life.

lines.speaker: Keef
lines.text: Little human is brave! I think I can offer you a deal...

lines.speaker: Keef
lines.text: If you can defeat me in battle, I will tell my men not to help the monkeys.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I accept your challenge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a fight!

lines.speaker: Keef
lines.text: Hahaha, human is funny! You will barely fill my stomach, stop wasting my time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nevermind.

lines.speaker: Keef
lines.text: Stop! Stop! You prove you are very strong, little human!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What about our deal?

lines.speaker: Keef
lines.text: A deal is a deal. We will not be allies with the monkeys.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have convinced the ogres and trolls not to form an alliance with the monkeys.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Excellent work, [player name].

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: I have had my men look into this supposed fleet of battleships. The shipyard has turned up nothing, although, someone did say they saw a familiar face sneaking around town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: An old pilot from the stronghold, he went by the name of Le Smith. I hadn't heard of him since he was dismissed from the glider regiment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right. He's the one who flew Glough here...

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: If that's the case then he's likely to have information we could use. You should track him down and see what you can learn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea where I should start looking?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Your guess is as good as mine - he must be somewhere on Ape Atoll.

sections.title: Talking to Assistant Le Smith (as Kruk)

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: Sorry, I don't have an amulet of monkey speak.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's okay, I am wearing my... uh... amulet of human speak.

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: Oh, right... What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was going to ask you the same question.

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: Glough told me not to talk to anyone while I'm here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're here with Glough?

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: I've already said too much. Please, leave me be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is that?

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: The fleet must be kept top secret... Agh! Forget I said anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Awowogei has already informed me about the battleships. In fact, he sent me here to check up on your progress.

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: So what is it you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the fleet? The shipyard still seems pretty bare.

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: Aha! The shipyard? Of course you aren't going to find anything there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aha... right... Of course?

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: The airships are being built just off the coast of Ape Atoll.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Airships!? I mean... Right.

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: Anyway, I have to get back to work. Let Awowogei know everything is on schedule.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I know enough already.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What fleet are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: I've already said too much. Please, leave me be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Awowogei has sent me to find Glough.

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: Well you're not going to find him up here. I'm afraid I'm too busy to chat right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was just admiring the view.

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: Me too, it's perfect conditions for my test flight.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good for you, I'll just be on my way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Test flight?

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: Oh sorry, ignore that. I just meant it's a lovely day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was just leaving.

sections.title: Talking to Assistant Le Smith (as yourself)

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Assistant Le Smith?

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: Yes? Wait... what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was going to ask you the same question.

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: Glough told me not to talk to anyone while I'm here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're here with Glough?

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: I've already said too much, human. Please, leave me be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is that?

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: The fleet must be kept top secret... Agh! Forget I said anything.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Awowogei has already informed me about the battleships. In fact, he sent me here to check up on your progress.

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: Odd, I didn't think Awowogei was the type to work with humans...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um.. I am a prisoner of war here, Awowogei gets me to run errands.

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: So what is it you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What fleet are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: I've already said too much, human. Please, leave me be.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: King Narnode has sent me to find Glough.

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: I'm afraid I have no idea what you're talking about... I'm just here on vacation!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've spoken to Assistant Le Smith.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: What have you learned?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He mentioned something about airships being built off the coast of Ape Atoll.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Airships? No wonder we didn't find anything at the shipyard... You'll have to do something about them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Firstly, you have to find out where they're constructing these airships...

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Once you've found the location, you'll have to find a way to sabotage the airships.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Monkey guard
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on here?

lines.speaker: Monkey guard
lines.text: I'm keeping watch on who comes and goes from the construction platform.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Construction platform?

lines.speaker: Monkey guard
lines.text: There's a large scale construction project taking place offshore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I visit the platform?

lines.speaker: Monkey guard
lines.text: I'm afraid only authorised personnel are allowed to visit the platform.

lines.speaker: Monkey guard
lines.text: Yes, sir. The engineers needed a large open space to construct the fleet, an offshore platform was the obvious solution.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the information.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I visit the platform?

lines.speaker: Monkey guard
lines.text: Of course, sir.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. See you around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was just passing through. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There must be some kind of explosive somewhere on this platform that I can use...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That should be enough, now I just have to get back to the boat...

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Have you found out any more about the 'secret weapon' Kruk was working on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet...

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Please hurry, [player name], we need to find out what we're up against here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've destroyed the airships!

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Excellent, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: While you were away, we managed to uncover something quite unsettling.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: We were investigating the tunnels where you found Kruk, and discovered an entrance to a much deeper cavern.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Unfortunately, we were unable to access the entrance ourselves, there was some magical force stopping us from getting through.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: We are fairly confident this is where the 'secret weapon' Kruk was working on is being developed, you'll have to find a way into the area.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you expect me to find a way through the magical barrier?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: I'm sure you'll find a way, [player name].

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Kruk! Where have you been? The experiments have broken out of their cages again, get them back under control before they destroy any more of my work!

sections.title: After defeating all the tortured gorillas

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Good work getting them back under control Kruk.

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Umesc pro ta lauf!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Clearly that last strain of the mutagen was too unstable...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mutagen?

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Right... I forgot I don't keep you around for your brains.

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: I've been experimenting on those specimens you've been gathering for me.

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: I've been trying to find a mutagen stable enough to allow me to have control over the creatures, whilst keeping them aggressive enough to tear apart anything that stands in our way.

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: I think I may have finally found the perfect balance, however, I will need time to carry out some final tests.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How big is this army of creatures?

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: I'm only using our test subjects at the moment, however, once I've processed the mutagen into an airborne form, I'll be able to unleash it across all of Ape Atoll!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: All that we have to do now is wait for the mutagen to infuse power from these charged onyx gems.

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: I'm going to find out where Le Smith has disappeared to, I'll let you know when I next require your services. Farewell, Kruk.

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Umesc pro ta andra!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should check what effect this has had on the mutagen.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Have you informed Awowogei of his failed plans?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Please hurry, [player name], we are so close to victory!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've taken care of Glough's secret weapon, he was working on some kind of mutagen for apes.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Good work, [player name]! Now all that's left to do is inform Awowogei of his failed plans. If he has any sense, he will call off his plans for attack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get to it right away.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: So, how is progress of our plans coming along?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I have bad news.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The trolls and ogres have backed out of our alliance...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The fleet of battleships will not be ready in time for our attack...

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: This is a disaster! What about your secret weapon?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Unfortunately there have been some complications, the weapon is still too unstable for use on the battlefield.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Agh! You have left me no choice but to cancel our assault on the mainland...

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: You have failed me for the last time, Kruk, you are hereby stripped of your rank and duties. Uwogo, cancel all of my upcoming meetings, I need some time alone.

lines.speaker: Uwogo
lines.text: You are not welcome here, Kruk.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: There's no time for chatting, [player name]! You need to get back to the Gnome Stronghold!

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Have you informed Awowogei of his failed plans?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, and he has called off the attack!

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: I knew you could do it, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: It would appear your mission on Ape Atoll is complete. You should inform the king that we've located Glough and thwarted Awowogei's plans. While you're ...

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Huh? What was that?

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: You said you dealt with the airships, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought I did!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Huaarghh! I knew I couldn't rely on these primitive apes, I'll just have to do this myself!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Le Smith, set our course for the Grand Tree, let's end this once and for all!

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: Yes, sir.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: We have to stop Glough! Get back to the Stronghold and warn the king before it's too late! I'll get the rest of the 10th squad together and we'll meet you there.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Get Nieve and defend the Stronghold, [player name]!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: How is the mission going, [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found Glough, he's headed for the Stronghold! We need to evacuate!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Evacuate? I think you're overreacting... My troops can handle Glough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't understand, he has an airship and army of demonic experiments!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: This is bad... I will put the troops on high alert. And... Well, we do have someone else who may be able to help...

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Nieve is the most highly skilled warrior we have here, get her assistance and defend the Stronghold.

sections.title: Talking to Nieve while she is following you

lines.speaker: Nieve
lines.text: Come on, [player name], we've got to defend the Stronghold!

sections.title: Leaving the Stronghold while Nieve is following you

lines.speaker: Nieve
lines.text: I'll meet you back at the Grand Tree, [player name].

sections.title: Talking to Nieve after killing enough tortured gorillas

lines.speaker: Nieve
lines.text: I think we've dealt with enough of these things, the troops can handle the rest! Let's join the 10th squad by the breach in the outer wall to the North.

lines.speaker: Nieve
lines.text: What's wrong?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glough is leading an attack on the Stronghold with an army of demonic creatures! We need your help!

lines.speaker: Nieve
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a long story, the king has requested that you join me in assisting the troops with the Stronghold's defence.

lines.speaker: Nieve
lines.text: I am unable to leave my post unattended, I'll have to bring in a replacement while I'm helping. Are you sure you want me to join you?

lines.speaker: Nieve
lines.text: You should pick up your pet first. It would only get in the way.

lines.speaker: Nieve
lines.text: What in Gielinor is that thing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's Glough's airship - and it's heading straight for the Grand Tree!

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: Sir, those experiments of yours are out of control back there, isn't there something you can do?

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Just focus on piloting the ship! Narnode isn't going to know what's hit him!

lines.speaker: Assistant Le Smith
lines.text: But sir, it's only a matter of time before they... Agghh!

lines.speaker: Nieve
lines.text: Huh? It completely missed the Grand Tree! We should head North to the crash site...

lines.speaker: Nieve
lines.text: Wait, look! Some of those things have broken into the Stronghold! We have to stop them, let's go!

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Good work, [player name], my troops can take it from here. You and Nieve should investigate the crash site.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll head there now.

sections.title: Talking to Lumo after killing enough tortured gorillas

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Good work dealing with those abominations, [player name], you should talk to the commander.

lines.speaker: Lumo
lines.text: Don't just stand there, [player name], help us deal with these abominations!

sections.title: Talking to Garkor after killing enough tortured gorillas

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: We'll keep the breach secure, [player name]. You should head to the crash site to make sure Glough is dealt with, once and for all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem, I'll finish this once and for all.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: I know you're tough, [player name], but it would be unwise to head out there on your own. Get Nieve and then come back.

lines.speaker: Garkor
lines.text: Some of those things have made it into the Stronghold, you've got to defeat them, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: You. Always it's you. Have you any idea how sick I am of you? What have you even got against me?

lines.speaker: Nieve
lines.text: Surrender to us, Glough, or I swear by my shiny blue shield that I will end you right here.

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Oh, will you? The lady talks tough. Let's see how you fare against my best pets!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Hahahaha!

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: Bah! Never trust a monkey to do a gnome's work.

lines.speaker: Glough
lines.text: I guess I'll just have to deal with you two meddling buffoons myself!

lines.speaker: Nieve
lines.text: He has the mutagen! We have to stop him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nieve, wait!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nooooooooo!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'll pay for this Glough!

lines.speaker: Carado
lines.text: Report back to the king as soon as possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh oh!

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: [player name], are you okay? We detected a very powerful presence in the caverns and decided to pull you out, I couldn't get a lock on Nieve, is she alright?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid she didn't make it... It was Glough, he injected himself with the demonic mutagen he had been developing... He was too powerful...

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: And what of Glough now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He's not a problem any longer.

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: I hope you are right, [player name].

lines.speaker: Zooknock
lines.text: You should report back to the king, I can teleport you to the Grand Tree if you would like?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glough has been defeated, and his demonic creations are trapped deep within the caverns to the North.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The monkeys were planning another attack, however, I managed to sabotage their plans.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: This is excellent news, [player name]. Where is Nieve?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid she didn't make it.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Her sacrifice will not be forgotten.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I have received word from monkey messengers that Awowogei himself wishes to make a visit to the Stronghold...

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Apparently he wishes to set up an agreement of peace between Ape Atoll and the mainland.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A peace treaty... Do you trust him?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: I don't have a choice. It would be foolish to decline such an offer, there may not be many more opportunities for peace between our kingdoms.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what now?

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Rest, [player name], you have earned it. I will have you summoned when the peace agreement is complete, you should be present during a moment of such historic importance.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: So, I hear you were the one to foil Glough's plans. Thank you, human, he had been setting our kingdoms against one another for far too long.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Finally, there can be peace between Ape Atoll and the mainland. [player name], you are welcome to visit Ape Atoll whenever you please, we will be opening our gates to the outside world for the first time.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Awowogei has allowed us to set up a Gnome glider route to Marim, you are welcome to use it as you wish.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: Please, feel free to talk to the new head of the Ape Atoll guard, Duke, about advanced combat training, should you wish to receive it.

lines.speaker: Awowogei
lines.text: I must be heading back now. Farewell, King Narnode, and thank you again, [player name].

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: On behalf of the Gnomes I can only thank you, [player name].

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Please accept this gift as a token of our gratitude for your services to our people.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: This is one of my personal seed pods, handed down through the generations of my family.

lines.speaker: King Narnode Shareen
lines.text: Simply commune with a pod and you'll be teleported right here to the Grand Tree.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thank you, King Narnode!
